Class Description:
AF Deconstructed provides modifications of the standard Animal Flow movements, making them more accessible to individuals who cannot perform the regular movements for any reason, whether that be lack of mobility and strength, injury, body mass, or age. In this 45-minute long class, Mike Fitch takes participants through a warm up, activations, and then learning and practicing a Flow. For each move, he'll demonstrate the regular move along with deconstructed, or regressed/modified variations, that you can perform instead.

This can serve as a great class to start out with if you don't feel ready for the standard movements; or this may offer the style class you want to stick with throughout your AF practice. It is up to you to decide if/when you will progress.

Animal Flow On Demand is intended for your personal use. If you'd like to further improve your individual skills by working one-on-one or taking a class with one of our Certified Instructors, check out our on-line directory to find one near you. See AnimalFlow.com for more information.

Class Title: Animal Flow - Free Sample Class (Beginner/ Entry Level)
Class Description:
Give Animal FlowÂ® a try, joining program creator Mike Fitch in this beginner-level sample class. Mike takes you through a 30-minute class, starting with some wrist mobilizations, and then teaching several introductory movements that you'll practice in drills and then build into a flow. Since this is a beginner class, each movement will be taught during the class, and you don't need any experience to jump in. This is a great way to see what the program is all about!

While this class introduces you to four of our Level 1 movements (Loaded Beast, Front Step Through, Underswitch, and Jumping Side Kickthrough), the full program includes 75 movements ranging from beginner to very advanced.

For more classes and flows, as well as tutorials for the entire library, try a FREE 14 day trial of our Animal Flow On Demand. You'll find beginner, intermediate and advanced levels so you can start at your own level and progress at your own pace. Using the app, you can work out from home, at the gym, in a park, or anywhere you want. With absolutely no equipment necessary, all you need is your body and some open space. New content is added all the time, and you'll find a brand new Flow of the Week every Monday!

Class Title: Full Crab Reach Tutorial Series: Introduction (Video 1)
Class Description:
The Full Crab Reach series includes 16 videos in 3 primary movement sections:

The Preparation Work tutorials are designed to help you warm up and prepare tissues for the demands on the Full Crab Reach. These are appropriate for all levels.

The Drills and Skills (Parts 1 and 2) tutorials are designed to be used at your own pace. Work through the drills that you have sufficient range of motion and control for. Practice them consistently until you are proficient and confident with them before adding in the next one.

Section 1: Preparation Work In the Preparation tutorials (Videos 2 â€“ 6), Master Instructor FrĂ©yja Spence takes you through a series of movements that focus on preparing the shoulders and spine for the demands of a Full Crab Reach. No matter your movement level, be sure to always start your Full Crab Reach practice with these preparatory movements in sequence. You can also use them individually or as a sequence within any flow practice warm up. For drills that are noted as stretches, hold them for 2 minutes.
For drills that are to be done as repetitions, aim to complete 4-6 (per side if itâ€™s a single-sided movement), for 2 to 3 total sets.

Section 2: Drills and Skills Part 1 In the first set of Drills and Skills tutorials (Videos 7 â€“ 11), Master Instructor FrĂ©yja Spence takes you through a series of drills that focus on training the spine extension, rotation, and coordination required but in standing and kneeling postures. You can continue with these drills AFTER you have completed the preparatory movements (Videos 2-6). You can use the 5 drills as a partial sequence (the first 2, 3, or 4) or in full (all 5 in a row) based on your current level. For all of the drills, complete 4-6 repetitions (per side if it is a single-sided movement), for 3-4 sets. Be sure to stop your practice if you are noticing a reduction in quality of movement or comfort.

Section 3: Drills and Skill Part 2 In the second set of Drills and Skills tutorials (Videos 12 - 16) Master Instructor FrĂ©yja Spence takes you through a series of drills that build the strength and coordination required for the Full movement. After beginning your Full Crab Reach practice with the Preparatory movements, and then continuing with a selection of drills from the previous Drills and Skills section, you can move into this last set of movements. You can use these last 5 drills as a partial sequence (selecting the first 2 or 3) or in full (all 5) based on your current level. For the first 3 drills requiring an isometric hold of the position, hold for 10-40 seconds and repeat for 3-5 sets. Ensure you can achieve 40 seconds in each drill before moving on to introduce the last two drills. For the last two drills requiring repetitions, complete 2-6 repetitions (per side if it is a single-sided movement), for 2-5 total sets. Be sure to stop your practice if you are noticing a reduction in quality of movement or comfort.

The beginner level Flow is drill that focuses on energy breaks and energy redirects. In AF we talk about an energy break as your ability to stop energy (i.e. your inertia or momentum), while a redirect is a change in the direction of energy. While the sequence is simple, utilizing just a Front Step and a Front Kickthrough, the challenge is in how well you can control your tempo and precision as you move through space. In addition to the workout suggestion below, this sequence is also awesome for times intervals so you may want to attempt sets of 30, 60, or 90 seconds in duration.

The Call Out:

Set Crab Left Leg Underswitch to Left Traveling Baboon into Left Side Rotating Ape into Right Leg Scorpion Sweep to Left Leg Reaching Underswitch to Right Leg Roll Through to Ape Leap to Right Leg Front Kickthrough into Left Leg Front Step Right Leg Reverse Traveling Underswitch Left Leg Reverse Traveling Underswitch Right Leg Side Kickthrough Right Leg Jumping Underswitch into Right Arm Crab Reach Return to Crab into Left Leg Jumping Underswitch into Right Side Pop Out Right Leg Scorpion Sweep into Left Leg Reaching Underswitch

If you plan on using this Flow as your workout, we recommend doing it in this order:

1- Begin with the short or long duration Wrist Mobilizations video in the Level 1 tutorial section and follow along.

2- Perform 10-15 repetitions of each movement for 2-3 sets. If you are unfamiliar with any of the movements, go to the Tutorial section and follow along before performing the previously suggested sets and reps.

3- Watch this weekâ€™s Flow and practice along.

4- Once you feel comfortable with the Flow, continue to practice, alternating between sides for your determined workout duration. *We would suggest 20-25 minutes for practicing the entire Flow.

5- Leave at least 3-5 minutes for an additional round of Wrist Mobilizations.

This interval class is considered Intermediate because it does use relatively simply movements, but still provides quite an intense workout. Class Title: Mike's Flow Class 37: Tuck Balance Conditioning Class
Class Description:
This 50-minute long conditioning class is focused on helping you build a strong foundation for your Tuck Balance practice. We don't actually attempt the Tuck Balance itself during the class. Instead, we'll go through a conditioning routines that includes lots of isometric holds, and exercises that focus on building your mobility and strength. Practicing this class on a regular basis can play an important role in helping you prepare to take on the skills practice needed to achieve your Tuck Balance. Check out the Tuck Balance Tutorial series for progressive exercises focused on the skills practice side of your Tuck Balance practice.

For this class, you'll need a pad or towel to place your knees on, as well as an object that is roughly chair height. You may also want to use some yoga blocks, foam roller, or other elevated surface.

In this 50-minute class you will be utilizing both fluid mobilizing movements and strength mobilizing movements. In the fluid mobility section, our goal is to move in and out of these ranges with as little tension as possible. In the Strength section we'll work on building strategic tension at end ranges of movement to build both strength and mobility. We'll start out slowly, and will work out way up to performing full sets of some Form Specific Stretches by the end of the class. This class is perfect as a warm up or cool down to any of the other Classes or Flows. No Animal Flow experience is necessary to enjoy this class.

This beginner level class from Master Instructor Richard Scrivener provides a full hour workout, including warm up, activations, conditioning and drill practice, and flow skills practice. You'll end the class by performing an entire flow that you learn during the class. This can be performed in any size space (you can always adjust your position if limited to a small space). Since this is a beginner level class , Mike will build each movement as you go along, providing detailed instructions on how to perform the movements. Therefore, no prior experience with Animal Flow is necessary. He will also begin by including the Wrist Mobilizations as part of your warm-up, so you do not need to do the Wrist Mobs videos first.

Note that once you've tried the full class, you can go to Flow Skills Class #33B Practice, in the Flow Skills Practice section, if you want to practice just the Flow portion of the class. That 33B variation includes just the Flow on repeat for 15 minutes.

The Flow for this class is: Set Crab Left Leg Jumping Underswitch into Right Leg Side Kickthrough Right Leg Underswitch into Ape High Hip Left Leg Reaching Underswitch Right Leg Jumping Underswitch Tap Left Leg Scorpion Switch Jump to Left Leg Front Kickthrough Pop back to Ape Reach Open Left Leg Reaching Underswitch (fake) Right Leg Reaching Underswitch into Left Arm Crab Reach Return to Crab into Right Leg Underswitch to Lateral Traveling Beast Lateral Traveling Beast (2 strides) Left Leg Underswitch

This is the "Practice" video companion to Rich's Flow Class #33A. You should do the full length class (Rich's Flow Class #33A - in the Classes: Flow Skills sub-category) first if possible, as that version teaches you each movement and takes you through the process of building the whole Flow. This 15-minute version contains only the complete Flow that you learn in the class. The video repeats the Flow several times, so that you can practice it for 15 minutes straight. This is a great option if you'd already done the full-length class and now just want to practice the Flow on repeat, and/or if you just want to get in a quick 15-minute workout without the warm-up and practice drills.

The Call Out for this Flow is: Set Crab Left Leg Jumping Underswitch into Right Leg Side Kickthrough Right Leg Underswitch into Ape High Hip Left Leg Reaching Underswitch Right Leg Jumping Underswitch Tap Left Leg Scorpion Switch Jump to Left Leg Front Kickthrough Pop back to Ape Reach Open Left Leg Reaching Underswitch (fake) Right Leg Reaching Underswitch into Left Arm Crab Reach Return to Crab into Right Leg Underswitch to Lateral Traveling Beast Lateral Traveling Beast (2 strides) Left Leg Underswitch

Mike Fitch provides a great drill for increasing strength and stability in a forward shoulder position. This position is seen in movements such as the end of the Forward Traveling Ape, the Loaded Beast Unload, or Beast Reach. Practicing this drill will help you with all of those moves!

Make sure you've learned the Front Kickthrough moves (from Level 1 Tutorials) before working on these technique drills.

Master Instructor Freyja Spence provides some great trips for perfecting your landing from the Full Scorpion (or Scorpion Switch). Be sure to learn the Full Scorpion and Scorpion Switch moves (from Level 1) before diving into these technical tips!
Mike Fitch provides a review of the Lateral Traveling Ape (Version 1) with some tips to improve your practice.
Mike Fitch teaches how to perform the Levitating Underswitch variation of the Underswitch.

Class Title: Welcome to Animal Flow On Demand
Class Description: None

Wrist Mobility exercises are one of the most important components of your practice. In this video Mike Fitch demonstrates several wrist and hand preparation exercises. You should be doing your wrist mobs before and after every practice. We recommend going through this entire 15-minute video whenever you can, and try to do at least 5 minute of wrist mobs before and after each practice.

Wrist Mobility exercises are one of the most important components of your practice. In this video Mike Fitch demonstrates several wrist mobility exercises. You should be doing your wrist mobs before and after every practice. This 9-minute video demonstrates several options that you can follow along with until you've learned to do them automatically. You can also check out the Long Version Wrist mobs for more extensive wrist and hand preparation.

In this 5-minute Wrist Mobilization video, Mike Fitch takes you through a 5-minute wrist warm-up that allows you to just follow along before or after you start your practice. This variation does not include any instructions, so be sure to check out one of the other Wrist Mobs videos (short and long variations) if you are just getting started and aren't familiar with how to perform these mobilizations. Mike spends 30 seconds on each variation/side, with a timer counting down your progress.
